message:
Command `# DRT-0302: VDDIO bterm has 16+ PORT geometries = "multiple pins".
# This is the padframe power rail appearing at multiple pad connection points.
# TritonRoute can't handle multi-port bterms.
# 
# SOLUTION: Use ODB Python script to consolidate VDDIO bterm to single PORT
# before DRT. This is cosmetic - the actual routing is already done in padframe.
#
# Check what other nets might have the same issue:
python3 -c "
with open('/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/32-openroad-globalrouting/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.def') as f:
    content = f.read()
import re
# Find all pin names in PINS section with multiple PORT entries
pins_section = re.search(r'PINS \d+.*?END PINS', content, re.DOTALL)
if pins_section:
    pin_text = pins_section.group(0)
    # Find pins with multiple PORTs
    pin_entries = re.split(r'\n    - ', pin_text)
    multi_port = []
    for entry in pin_entries[1:]:  # skip header
        name_match = re.match(r'(\S+)', entry)
        port_count = entry.count('+ PORT')
        if name_match and port_count > 1:
            multi_port.append((name_match.group(1), port_count))
    print('Pins with multiple PORTs:')
    for name, count in sorted(multi_port, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:20]:
        print(f'  {name}: {count} ports')
"` executed with exit code 0.

function.name: execute_bash

content:
Pins with multiple PORTs:
  VDDIO: 24 ports
  VSSIO: 24 ports

extras.command:
# DRT-0302: VDDIO bterm has 16+ PORT geometries = "multiple pins".
# This is the padframe power rail appearing at multiple pad connection points.
# TritonRoute can't handle multi-port bterms.
# 
# SOLUTION: Use ODB Python script to consolidate VDDIO bterm to single PORT
# before DRT. This is cosmetic - the actual routing is already done in padframe.
#
# Check what other nets might have the same issue:
python3 -c "
with open('/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/pnr_run_v4/32-openroad-globalrouting/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper.def') as f:
    content = f.read()
import re
# Find all pin names in PINS section with multiple PORT entries
pins_section = re.search(r'PINS \d+.*?END PINS', content, re.DOTALL)
if pins_section:
    pin_text = pins_section.group(0)
    # Find pins with multiple PORTs
    pin_entries = re.split(r'\n    - ', pin_text)
    multi_port = []
    for entry in pin_entries[1:]:  # skip header
        name_match = re.match(r'(\S+)', entry)
        port_count = entry.count('+ PORT')
        if name_match and port_count > 1:
            multi_port.append((name_match.group(1), port_count))
    print('Pins with multiple PORTs:')
    for name, count in sorted(multi_port, key=lambda x: -x[1])[:20]:
        print(f'  {name}: {count} ports')
"
